description_long: This object is a compact, antique-style shotgun with a short, wide barrel made of dark, ornately engraved metal. The barrel features subtle floral engravings and a thick muzzle, suggesting a design from the 19th century or earlier. The grip is crafted from dark, polished wood, shaped ergonomically to fit the hand, and is attached to a metal frame with a visible trigger and a rounded trigger guard. A prominent hammer is located above the grip, indicating a single-shot, break-action mechanism. The left side of the frame displays a fan-shaped metal plate, adding to the decorative and functional aspects of the firearm. The overall build is robust, with a blend of metal and wood materials, giving it a historical and utilitarian appearance.
description: A compact, ornately engraved antique shotgun with a short metal barrel and a dark wooden grip. Features a prominent hammer and decorative metalwork.
description_view_0: Front view showing the wide, circular muzzle opening and the alignment of the barrel.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the engraved barrel, wooden grip, and exposed hammer.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the wooden grip's width and the alignment of the hammer and trigger guard.
description_view_3: Right side view showing the fan-shaped metal plate, trigger guard, and detailed engravings on the barrel.
attribute_barrel_configuration: single-barrel, short and wide with no visible ribbing or venting
attribute_trigger_guard_design: rounded, medium-sized trigger guard with a smooth contour and no finger grooves
